milestone: LoRa first light (#21/#22) — DFR1195 SX1262 bring-up
runner: hive; 2 tuxedo DFR1195 (D1 480e900e, D2 2cab5f69), nobt,lora,loratcxo, core's r2-sx1262 driver, via ssh tuxedo serial
hardware: DFR1195 (FireBeetle 2 ESP32-S3) + SX1262; SPI3 SCK7/MISO5/MOSI6, NSS10, RST41, BUSY40, RXEN42, DIO1=4; TCXO DIO3 1.8V

expectations.observed: configure() OK + RF radiated/received = DIO3 TCXO 1.8V locks the PLL (core's #1 confirm: TCXO=yes, .with_tcxo(V1_8) required)
both boards transmit AND receive LoRa frames = RF works end to end
D2 RX from=480e900e (rssi -44dBm snr 12dB); D1 RX from=2cab5f69 (rssi -45dBm snr 13dB) — bidirectional, clean payload (8B hive id decoded), every cycle
DIO2-as-RF-switch (autonomous antenna) + RXEN42 (MCU HIGH-RX/LOW-TX) complementary, per core — clean TX and RX confirm the polarity
core's r2-sx1262 configure/transmit/listen/poll(RadioEvent)/read(RxInfo) all functional on metal; RSSI/SNR readout valid

notes: FIRST LIGHT on the pilot-site radio (#21/#22). Bidirectional LoRa between two DFR1195 SX1262 radios via core's r2-sx1262 driver + hive's esp-hal wiring (SPI3 + GPIO + TCXO). The one-codebase north-star holds for LoRa too: shared chip-agnostic driver + thin per-platform radio wiring. BENCH NOTES (artifacts, not defects, for the record): (1) SF12 (the pilot-site deployment SF) gave per-packet CRC-err at 30cm because an 8B SF12 frame is ~2s ToA vs the ~3s listen/TX windows -> the RX catches PARTIAL frames -> CRC fails; SF7 (~40ms ToA) -> clean (a timing/half-duplex bench artifact, NOT an RF/driver fault). (2) tx_power -9dBm for the 30cm bench (deployment uses +20/+22 for 18km). NEXT: drop the manual RXEN toggle for core's incoming RXEN driver param; LoRa MESH (bridge io_task PCO+routing to the LoRa carrier); then SF12 real-distance range test + cross-transport LoRa<->WiFi gateway (DG-2 #16, HBSYNC-07).
